text.title: Gce Spot Vms Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the gce-spot-vms methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Spot/preemptible workloads MUST persist progress checkpoints to Cloud Storage at intervals shorter than the expected preemption window.
rule.rationale: Without checkpointing, preemption wipes hours of work; periodic checkpoint ensures bounded re-execution cost.

rule.statement: Workloads on Spot VMs MUST handle the 30-second `SIGTERM` shutdown notice and flush state.
rule.rationale: Spot VMs receive a 30-second notice; ignoring it loses uncommitted work and may corrupt downstream state.

rule.statement: Mixed MIGs MUST keep a non-Spot baseline (≥1 instance) for SLO floor + Spot for burst.
rule.rationale: Pure-Spot fleets risk full preemption; mixed pools maintain availability while harvesting cost savings.

rule.statement: Spot VMs in MIGs MUST set `--instance-termination-action=STOP` (or `DELETE`) consistently; mixing in one MIG is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mismatched termination actions inside a MIG produce inconsistent recovery behaviour.

rule.statement: Workloads with SLOs above 99.5% availability MUST NOT run on Spot/preemptible alone.
rule.rationale: Spot availability is best-effort; relying on Spot for critical SLOs makes the SLO unattainable.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
